name: Corncakes
recipeIngredient: Pinch sugar (omit this if you use sweet corn)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil over high heat and plunge the corn into it. Cover and when the water returns to a boil, turn off the heat. Let the corn sit for 5 minutes.
2. Remove the corn and rinse in cold water. Scrape the kernels off the cob and put them into the work bowl of a food processor.
3. Add the eggs and flour and process in short pulses until the mixture is still coarse but even in color and well combined, incorporating the sugar, cumin, and salt.
4. Spray a non-stick skillet with Pam and heat until a bit of batter dropped on the surface bubbles in the center and browns on the bottom.
5. Pour about 3 tablespoons of batter into the pan. When bubbles form on the surface and the bottom is browned, about 3 to 4 minutes, flip the corncake to brown on the other side.
6. When done, transfer the corncake to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm. Repeat until all the batter has been used.
These are delicious served plain or with salsa and a dollop of fat-free sour cream.
